### try this 10/3 with more than 4 decimals

- **From:** Carlos lages <member129@dbforums.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-22T23:09:17+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2674193.1048374557@dbforums.com>`

```

I am Using Power 6.1
and really dont work
try to do this
create a Forms
Create OK  buttom
and  in Ckick Event
work storage
01  ws-campo  pic 9(04)v9999999  value zeros.

procedure.

   Compute  ws-campo = 10 / 3
   move  ws-campo   to  Pow-Numeric of  wcampo    (a field in a Forms)

You will see only   3,3333  and not 3,333333333

in other Word you can not have more than 4 digits after decimal

is there a solutions for this

Use a Standard Render Text  doenst  work, because you can not
Limit the User to type  1234,567890 instead  1,234567890


Carlos Lages
```

#### ↳ Re: try this 10/3 with more than 4 decimals

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-22T19:31:00-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Ft6dnWl1pO18k-CjXTWciQ@giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<2674193.1048374557@dbforums.com>`

```

"Carlos lages" <member129@dbforums.com>
>
> Use a Standard Render Text  doenst  work, because you can not
> Limit the User to type  1234,567890 instead  1,234567890
>

Yes you can.

MOVE 'TEXT' OF TXT-FIELD TO DUMMYX20.
COMPUTE TEST-VALUE = NUMVAL(DUMMYX20).
IF TEST-VALUE < 2
    PERFORM ERROR-ROUTINE
    MOVE SPACE TO 'TEXT' OF TXT-FIELD
    INVOKE TXT-FIELD 'SetFocus'
END-IF

Treat these crazy cases as you would a funny part number ("AA123BB567").
There's no built-in way to validate some things as the user types (part
numbers, colors, postal codes, etc.) - you have to do it the hard way.
```

#### ↳ Re: try this 10/3 with more than 4 decimals

- **From:** "Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2003-03-23T02:23:27
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3e7d1aa3_1@text-west.newsgroups.com>`
- **References:** `<2674193.1048374557@dbforums.com>`

```
Carlos,

I can see you are upset about this so I did as you asked, and set up a
project under PowerCOBOL 6.1.

You are correct.

It does only show 4 decimals.

But there are very sound reasons for this which you have overlooked. As I
remarked earlier, you are missing something here.

POW-NUMERIC can only be used in two cases:

1. With a ListView (where you want elements of the list to be numeric. In
this case it's use must be subscripted to indicate the level of the list you
are referring to...).
2. With the Fujitsu EXCEL control where you are exchanging information with
EXCEL via OLE.

In both of the above cases this Property (POW-NUMERIC) has an OLE type of
VT_CY.

That means it is intended to be a fixed point currency field.

Fixed point currency fields are ALWAYS pic s9(14)v9(4) in their COBOL
equivalence. So if you use POW-NUMERIC, the system correctly assumes you are
using fixed point currency and shows 4 decimal places.

In other words, POW-NUMERIC has an implicit definition of s9(14)v9(4). You
cannot change it.

The solution is NOT to use POW-NUMERIC.

Bill Klein posted a redefinition solution which is the one I would also have
used.

Consider this...

When you do internal calculations that must be sent to a report you normally
edit them with an edit string, before printing, right?

Exactly the same consideration should apply to your screen output. Treat the
data as a string and you will have no problems.
If you read the article on components I wrote in www.aboutlegacycoding.com
(Components101) this month, it will tell you where to download a component
that does exactly what you need, with input and output strings on screens. I
have put this component in the public domain and it is free. (I have already
had feedback from several people who are using it and they like it.) You can
use it in your Windows Applications, in your Client and server Web
applications and in inter-program communication to verify that strings are
numeric and all the details about how many numbers on each side of the
point, etc. The component has properties that provide the number in about 6
different useful formats, including the standard currency format.

HTH,

Pete.

"Carlos lages" <member129@dbforums.com> wrote in message
news:2674193.1048374557@dbforums.com...

…[26 more quoted lines elided]…
> Posted via http://dbforums.com

```

[peter

After a Hard work I found out a easy (not so easy) way
to do that.

 ENVIRONMENT     DIVISION.
 DATA            DIVISION.
 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

 01 WS-campox    PIC X(11) IS GLOBAL.
 01 F REDEFINES WS-campox  IS GLOBAL.
    05 WS-campo PIC 9999,999999.

 PROCEDURE       DIVISION.
     MOVE POW-TEXT   OF wCAMPO    TO WS-campox.
     move  ws-campo  to Pow-Text  of wcampo1.


* wcampo and wcampo1 are fields  in a Forms
* with RenderStyle 1-CobolPicture  zzz9,999999.

* kind of field.

this  works fine
i will check your Componnet

## Thread: [OT] A Strange, Unbidden Thought — 5 msgs

### [OT] A Strange, Unbidden Thought

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2003-03-23T10:19:46-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b5kjai$otl$1@panix1.panix.com>`

```


Warning - this posting relates a thought I had which some might interpret
as making light of a miracle related in the New Testament (Matt.xiv 17-21,
Mark.vi 38-44, Luke.ix 13-17, John.vi 9-13 or thereabouts); those who are 
offended by such things are advised to go, now - perhaps to sin no more? - 
but you'll miss a joke that I think you could tell your 
(pastor/priest/reverend/etc) and raise a smile.

Yesterday, after taking lunch at the 'Don't Speak Our Language?  Screw 
You' shopping-mall in Fall's Church, VA (known to the locals as the Eden 
Center and a hub of the area's Vietnamese commerce) I was driving up the 
highway, just north of Washington, DC and I saw a bus... a recent-model, 
clean, nicely-painted bus emblazoned with the logo of 'Bob and Edna's 
Bible Tours'.

Now... my memory is, admittedly, porous, and I might not be recalling the 
names exactly right... but it was *somebody's* 'Bible Tours'.  Leaving 
aside the incongruity of having a Bible Tour pass so closely to the 
obviously Godless environs of Washington, DC I had a moderately disturbing 
thought which sprang from my own memories of bus-tours from decades past.

Long, weary jostlings over endless miles... and then, to alleviate the 
tedium, somebody would start one of those Songs to Alleviate Bus-Tedium 
that so many folks know... and then, it came to me, one such Song to 
Alleviate Bus-Tedium that might be sung by the group on Bob and Edna's 
Bible Tour...

Five loaves of bread and two fish on the wall,
Five loaves of bread and two fish!
You take some down and pass 'em around,
Five loaves of bread and two fish on the wall!
```

#### ↳ Re: [OT] A Strange, Unbidden Thought

> tedium, somebody would start one of those Songs to Alleviate Bus-Tedium
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…

Trust me, that was not the song you sang in your youth.

the post) and do a print summary of some percentages of various GPA's. I
can't get it to compile in Net Express I get these erros:

be numeric

be numeric
   162          COMPUTE ENG-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE

be numeric
   196     WRITE OUTPUT-FILE.
"C:\My Documents\COBOL\a-7-dj.cbl"(196,18): * 330-S Not a record name
* Total Messages:     4
* Unrecoverable :     0                    Severe  :     4
* Errors        :     0                    Warnings:     0
* Informational :     0                    Flags   :     0
* Data:        1438     Code:           0
* Checking complete - errors found

Are my 88's wrong? Is my logic ok in the code? Are my If statments ok or
should I do then another way. Can somebody please help, I've been working on
this for a while. I'm new to this, so any help would be greatly appricated.

Thank you very much,
Dan



      IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. A-7-DJ.
       AUTHOR. DANNY J.
       INSTALLATION.
       DATE-WRITTEN. 3-17-3.
       DATE-COMPILED.
      ***************************************************
      *  ASSIGMENT #7 - DECISION MAKING                 *
      ***************************************************

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
       FILE-CONTROL.
           SELECT STUDENT-FILE  ASSIGN TO 'C0805.DAT'
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
           SELECT OUTPUT-FILE ASSIGN TO 'OUTPUT.RPT'
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       FD  STUDENT-FILE.
       01  STUDENT-REC.
           05  SOC-SEC-NO                      PIC X(9).
           05  STU-NAME                        PIC X(21).
           05  CLS-CODE                        PIC X.
               88 FRESHMAN                     VALUE '1'.
               88 SOPHOMORE                    VALUE '2'.
               88 JUNIOR                       VALUE '3'.
               88 SENIOR                       VALUE '4'.
           05  SCL-CODE                        PIC X.
               88 BUSINESS                     VALUE '1'.
               88 LIBERALARTS                  VALUE '2'.
               88 ENGINEERING                  VALUE '3'.
           05  GPA                             PIC 9(3)V99.
           05  CRD-EARNED                      PIC 9(3).

       FD  OUTPUT-FILE
           RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS.
       01    OUT-SALARY-REC                    PIC X(80).
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01  LINE-CT                             PIC 99     VALUE 0.
       01  ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS              PIC XXX    VALUE 'YES'.

      *01  ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS              PIC XXX  VALUE 'YES'.
      *    88 NO-MORE-RECORDS                           VALUE 'NO '.

       01  WS-DATE.
           05 WS-YEAR                          PIC 9999.
           05 WS-MONTH                         PIC 99.
           05 WS-DAY                           PIC 99.
       01  DETAIL-LINE.
           05                                  PIC X(4)   VALUE SPACES.
           05                                  PIC X(5)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  LOWR-THAN-TWO                   PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  BTN-TWO-AND-THREE               PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  GRT-THEN-THREE                  PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  BUS-MJR                         PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  LIB-ARTS-MJR                    PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  ENG-MJR                         PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  MORE-THAN-HUND                  PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  FRHMEN                          PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  SPHMORS                         PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  JUNRS                           PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  SENRS                           PIC 9(2)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  GPA-SUB-TOTAL                   PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  TOTAL-SCL                       PIC 9(3)V99.

       01  HDR-1.
           05                                  PIC X(15)  VALUE SPACES.
           05                                  PIC X(26)
                 VALUE 'PRINT SUMMARY OF DATA %'.
           05                                  PIC X(22)  VALUE SPACES.
           05  DATE-OUT.
               10 MONTH-OUT                    PIC XX.
               10                              PIC X      VALUE '/'.
               10 DAY-OUT                      PIC XX.
               10                              PIC X      VALUE '/'.
               10 YEAR-OUT                     PIC XXXX.
           05                                  PIC X(11).
           05                                  PIC X(5)   VALUE 'PAGE'.
           05  PAGE-NO                         PIC 99     VALUE ZERO.
       01  HDR-2.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05                                  PIC X(25)
                VALUE 'GPA<2%  GPA>2%&<3  GPA>3%'.
           05                                  PIC X(46)
                VALUE 'GPA>3% FOR BUSSINES  LIBERAL ARTS  ENGINEERING'.
       01  HDR-3.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05                                  PIC X(25).
           05                                  PIC X(23)
                VALUE '>100 CREDITS AND GPS<2%'.
           05                                  PIC X(46)
                VALUE 'GPA>3%  FRESHMAN  SOPHOMORES  JUNIORS  SENIORS'.

       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
      ****************************************************
      *   Main module controls overall program logic     *
      ****************************************************
       100-MAIN-MODULE.
           OPEN INPUT  STUDENT-FILE
                OUTPUT OUTPUT-FILE
      *    MOVE SPACES TO OUTPUT-FILE
           PERFORM UNTIL ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS = 'NO '
               READ STUDENT-FILE
                   AT END
                      MOVE 'NO ' TO ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS
                   NOT AT END
                      PERFORM 200-GPA-RTN
               END-READ
           END-PERFORM
           CLOSE STUDENT-FILE
                 OUTPUT-FILE
           STOP RUN.
      ****************************************************
      *    Performed from 100-MAIN-MODULE, this routine  *
      ****************************************************

       200-GPA-RTN.
      ****************************************************
      * GPA'S % OF <2, >2 AND <3 AND >3 DECISION         *
      ****************************************************
           IF  GPA < 2
           THEN
               COMPUTE LOWR-THAN-TWO = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
           IF  GPA > 2  AND < 3
           THEN
               COMPUTE BTN-TWO-AND-THREE = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
           IF  GPA > 3
           THEN
               COMPUTE GRT-THEN-THREE= GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
      ******************************************************
      *  % OF STUDENTS WITH GPA'S GREATER THAN 3.0 IN      *
      *  BUS, LIB ARTS, ENG.                               *
      ******************************************************
           MOVE GRT-THEN-THREE TO TOTAL-SCL.

           IF SCL-CODE = '1'
           THEN
               COMPUTE BUS-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
           END-IF
           IF SCL-CODE = '2'
           THEN
               COMPUTE LIB-ARTS-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
           END-IF
           IF SCL-CODE = '3'
           THEN
                COMPUTE ENG-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
           END-IF

      ********************************************************
      *   STUDENTS WITH MORE THAN 100 CREDITS AND HAVE A GPA *
      *   OF LESS THAN 2.0%                                  *
      ********************************************************
           IF GPA > 2 AND CRD-EARNED < 100
           THEN
               COMPUTE MORE-THAN-HUND = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
      ********************************************************
      *    % OF STUDENTS WITH GPA GREATER THAN 3.0 WHO ARE   *
      *    FRESHMAN, SOPHOMORES, JUNNIORS, AND SENIORS       *
      ********************************************************
           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE= '1'
           THEN
               COMPUTE FRHMEN = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF

           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE = '2'
           THEN
               COMPUTE SPHMORS = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF

           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE= '3'
           THEN
               COMPUTE JUNRS = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF

           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE = '4'
           THEN
               COMPUTE SENRS = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
           WRITE OUTPUT-FILE.

 The data file ('C0805.DAT'):

234789543P. MARTINSON         11250011
276888003M. JACKSON           22394020
332557267B. BRUHANSKY         21314020

122886567F. DARK              41297034
224257889S. SMITH             41397038
125654334C. HAYES             41400040
345345669J. FISHER            42200036
432234543S. JONES             43291033
488345612M. PILLION           41255034

#### ↳ Re: Question

- **From:** Frederico Fonseca <real-email-in-msg-spam@email.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-23T23:19:12+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<o3gs7v0839mto4k605e39l0lm6r5lc1mgo@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<c4nfa.38249$q26.2070134@read1.cgocable.net>`

```
On Sun, 23 Mar 2003 13:36:59 -0500, "Dan J" <dali@cogeco.ca> wrote:

>Hello,
>
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>be numeric
Variables in a compute (or any aritmetic expression) must be numeric.
 (SCL-CODE is a PIC X and therefore can not be used)

>   196     WRITE OUTPUT-FILE.
>"C:\My Documents\COBOL\a-7-dj.cbl"(196,18): * 330-S Not a record name
You do not write the file, you write the record.

Hope the above is clear.

ff
Frederico Fonseca
ema il: frederico _ fonseca at syssoft-int . com
```

#### ↳ Re: Question

- **From:** robert@wagner.net (Robert Wagner)
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T00:45:42+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3e7e546b.47140072@news.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<c4nfa.38249$q26.2070134@read1.cgocable.net>`

```
"Dan J" <dali@cogeco.ca> wrote:

>Hello,
>
…[29 more quoted lines elided]…
>this for a while. I'm new to this, so any help would be greatly appricated.

Compiler messages are clear. You cannot divide by letters (pic x), only by
numbers (pic 9).

There are SERIOUS structural errors in your program. You want a
three-dimensional table where one dimension is major (SCL, 3),  one is year (4),
and the third is grade triad (3). These are all accumulators to which you will
add 1. At the first and second dimension, you also want to accumulate the total
of next-level subordinates. Don't try to accumulate in DETAIL-RECORD. That's an
output format, not internal accumulators. Change the PICs in detail-record to
edited (zzz.99), and redesign the report to print three lines, one per SCL (it
appears that's what you want), with a description of the SCL. If you want a
one-line report, leave it as is. Get rid of 88s because you're not using them
anyway. The formula for percentage (do remember to say rounded) is (numerator *
100) / denominator. Do not say (numerator / denominator) * 100 because you'll
lose precision. 

In summary, learn to use tables (OCCURS) and subscripts for both accumulators
and report columns. 

Feel free to post here if you have trouble with an IMPROVED versions of your
program. We like to see the student is making an effort rather than asking us to
do the work for him. Why? Because in the real world, you don't get helpful
hints, you get fired. That can bum you out for a whole weekend. 

Robert


>      IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
>       PROGRAM-ID. A-7-DJ.
…[213 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Question

- **From:** Joe Zitzelberger <joe_zitzelberger@nospam.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T09:32:22-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<joe_zitzelberger-D8B98A.09322224032003@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<c4nfa.38249$q26.2070134@read1.cgocable.net>`

```
In article <c4nfa.38249$q26.2070134@read1.cgocable.net>,
 "Dan J" <dali@cogeco.ca> wrote:

> * Copyright (C) 1984-1999 Micro Focus Ltd.     URN AXCGG/AA0/00000
>    154         COMPUTE BUS-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> be numeric

and

>            IF SCL-CODE = '1'
>            THEN
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>            END-IF

SCL-CODE is not defined as a numeric value, but as a 1-byte text string.  
You will need to do some sort of type cast to get this into a numeric 
value, or use the "Function NumVal( SCL-CODE )" in your compute 
statements.



>    196     WRITE OUTPUT-FILE.
> "C:\My Documents\COBOL\a-7-dj.cbl"(196,18): * 330-S Not a record name

>            WRITE OUTPUT-FILE.

This is part of that freaky and not very successful effort to make Cobol 
easy to code for english speaking non-programmers.  The idea is that one 
would say "READ the-file" but "WRITE the-record".  So in the write 
statement, the file that is being written to is never mentioned.  This 
is just unusual and there really isn't a good way to learn this, you 
just have to get bitten by it a few times to remember.  Your statement 
should look like:

>            WRITE OUT-SALARY-REC.




> 
> Are my 88's wrong?

Your 88s are good, but you don't seem to be using them.  For example, 
SCL-CODE is defined with:

>            05  SCL-CODE                        PIC X.
>                88 BUSINESS                     VALUE '1'.
>                88 LIBERALARTS                  VALUE '2'.
>                88 ENGINEERING                  VALUE '3'.

but you have IF statements checking for "1" and "2" and "3".  This 
defeats the purpose of defining the 88s.  You might want to go back and 
try:

   IF BUSINESS
      stuff
   END-IF
   IF LIBERALARTS
      stuff
   END-IF
   IF ENGINEERING
      stuff
   END-IF


The same holds true for ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS.  You could "SET 
NO-MORE-RECORDS to true" and "PERFORM UNTIL NO-MORE-RECORDS".

>            PERFORM UNTIL ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS = 'NO '
>                READ STUDENT-FILE
>                    AT END
>                       MOVE 'NO ' TO ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS
>                    NOT AT END



   
>Are my If statments ok or should I do then another way.

The are ok, and they work, but in some cases, like the above, of several 
mutually exclusive conditions EVALUATE is a nice tool.


> Is my logic ok in the code?

The true test of that is the execution of the program.  Does it function 
properly?

Regards,

Joe Zitzelberger
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Question

- **From:** robert@wagner.net (Robert Wagner)
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T17:29:08+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3e7f3f73.16928357@news.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<c4nfa.38249$q26.2070134@read1.cgocable.net> <joe_zitzelberger-D8B98A.09322224032003@corp.supernews.com>`

```
Joe Zitzelberger <joe_zitzelberger@nospam.com> wrote:

>In article <c4nfa.38249$q26.2070134@read1.cgocable.net>,
> "Dan J" <dali@cogeco.ca> wrote:

>>    196     WRITE OUTPUT-FILE.
>> "C:\My Documents\COBOL\a-7-dj.cbl"(196,18): * 330-S Not a record name
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>>            WRITE OUT-SALARY-REC.

The reason we write records is not to make the language english-like; we do it
to specify the record length. An FD could have three records with different
lengths. If we wrote the file, the compiler wouldn't know which record length to
use. Note that DELETE does use the file name.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Question

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T09:50:14-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b5nggm$2j43$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<c4nfa.38249$q26.2070134@read1.cgocable.net> <joe_zitzelberger-D8B98A.09322224032003@corp.supernews.com> <3e7f3f73.16928357@news.optonline.net>`

```

"Robert Wagner" <robert@wagner.net> wrote in message
news:3e7f3f73.16928357@news.optonline.net...
> If we wrote the file, the compiler wouldn't know which record length to
use.

ISO/IEC 1989:2002 allows "WRITE FILE <filename> ..." as well as REWRITE FILE
<filename> ...".   The FROM phrase is required in these contexts and
determines the description of the record.

    -Chuck Stevens
```

---

the post) and do a print summary of some percentages of various GPA's. I
can't get it to compile in Net Express I get these erros:

Micro Focus NetExpress V3
Version 3.0.14 Copyright (C) 1984-1999 Micro Focus Ltd.
URN AXCGG/AA0/00000
* Micro Focus NetExpress UE          V3.0 revision 255 Compiler
* Copyright (C) 1984-1999 Micro Focus Ltd.     URN AXCGG/AA0/00000

"C:\My Documents\COBOL\a-7-dj.cbl"(154,44): *  34-S Operand SCL-CODE should
be numeric
   158         COMPUTE LIB-ARTS-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
"C:\My Documents\COBOL\a-7-dj.cbl"(158,49): *  34-S Operand SCL-CODE should
be numeric
   162          COMPUTE ENG-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
"C:\My Documents\COBOL\a-7-dj.cbl"(162,45): *  34-S Operand SCL-CODE should
be numeric
   196     WRITE OUTPUT-FILE.
"C:\My Documents\COBOL\a-7-dj.cbl"(196,18): * 330-S Not a record name
* Total Messages:     4
* Unrecoverable :     0                    Severe  :     4
* Errors        :     0                    Warnings:     0
* Informational :     0                    Flags   :     0
* Data:        1438     Code:           0
* Checking complete - errors found

Are my 88's wrong? Is my logic ok in the code? Are my If statments ok or
should I do then another way. Can somebody please help, I've been working on
this for a while. I'm new to this, so any help would be greatly appricated.

Thank you very much,
Dan



      IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. A-7-DJ.
       AUTHOR. DANNY J.
       INSTALLATION.
       DATE-WRITTEN. 3-17-3.
       DATE-COMPILED.
      ***************************************************
      *  ASSIGMENT #7 - DECISION MAKING                 *
      ***************************************************

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
       FILE-CONTROL.
           SELECT STUDENT-FILE  ASSIGN TO 'C0805.DAT'
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
           SELECT OUTPUT-FILE ASSIGN TO 'OUTPUT.RPT'
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       FD  STUDENT-FILE.
       01  STUDENT-REC.
           05  SOC-SEC-NO                      PIC X(9).
           05  STU-NAME                        PIC X(21).
           05  CLS-CODE                        PIC X.
               88 FRESHMAN                     VALUE '1'.
               88 SOPHOMORE                    VALUE '2'.
               88 JUNIOR                       VALUE '3'.
               88 SENIOR                       VALUE '4'.
           05  SCL-CODE                        PIC X.
               88 BUSINESS                     VALUE '1'.
               88 LIBERALARTS                  VALUE '2'.
               88 ENGINEERING                  VALUE '3'.
           05  GPA                             PIC 9(3)V99.
           05  CRD-EARNED                      PIC 9(3).

       FD  OUTPUT-FILE
           RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS.
       01    OUT-SALARY-REC                    PIC X(80).
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01  LINE-CT                             PIC 99     VALUE 0.
       01  ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS              PIC XXX    VALUE 'YES'.

      *01  ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS              PIC XXX  VALUE 'YES'.
      *    88 NO-MORE-RECORDS                           VALUE 'NO '.

       01  WS-DATE.
           05 WS-YEAR                          PIC 9999.
           05 WS-MONTH                         PIC 99.
           05 WS-DAY                           PIC 99.
       01  DETAIL-LINE.
           05                                  PIC X(4)   VALUE SPACES.
           05                                  PIC X(5)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  LOWR-THAN-TWO                   PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  BTN-TWO-AND-THREE               PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  GRT-THEN-THREE                  PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  BUS-MJR                         PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  LIB-ARTS-MJR                    PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  ENG-MJR                         PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  MORE-THAN-HUND                  PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  FRHMEN                          PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  SPHMORS                         PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  JUNRS                           PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  SENRS                           PIC 9(2)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  GPA-SUB-TOTAL                   PIC 9(3)V99.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05  TOTAL-SCL                       PIC 9(3)V99.

       01  HDR-1.
           05                                  PIC X(15)  VALUE SPACES.
           05                                  PIC X(26)
                 VALUE 'PRINT SUMMARY OF DATA %'.
           05                                  PIC X(22)  VALUE SPACES.
           05  DATE-OUT.
               10 MONTH-OUT                    PIC XX.
               10                              PIC X      VALUE '/'.
               10 DAY-OUT                      PIC XX.
               10                              PIC X      VALUE '/'.
               10 YEAR-OUT                     PIC XXXX.
           05                                  PIC X(11).
           05                                  PIC X(5)   VALUE 'PAGE'.
           05  PAGE-NO                         PIC 99     VALUE ZERO.
       01  HDR-2.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05                                  PIC X(25)
                VALUE 'GPA<2%  GPA>2%&<3  GPA>3%'.
           05                                  PIC X(46)
                VALUE 'GPA>3% FOR BUSSINES  LIBERAL ARTS  ENGINEERING'.
       01  HDR-3.
           05                                  PIC X(2)   VALUE SPACES.
           05                                  PIC X(25).
           05                                  PIC X(23)
                VALUE '>100 CREDITS AND GPS<2%'.
           05                                  PIC X(46)
                VALUE 'GPA>3%  FRESHMAN  SOPHOMORES  JUNIORS  SENIORS'.

       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
      ****************************************************
      *   Main module controls overall program logic     *
      ****************************************************
       100-MAIN-MODULE.
           OPEN INPUT  STUDENT-FILE
                OUTPUT OUTPUT-FILE
      *    MOVE SPACES TO OUTPUT-FILE
           PERFORM UNTIL ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS = 'NO '
               READ STUDENT-FILE
                   AT END
                      MOVE 'NO ' TO ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS
                   NOT AT END
                      PERFORM 200-GPA-RTN
               END-READ
           END-PERFORM
           CLOSE STUDENT-FILE
                 OUTPUT-FILE
           STOP RUN.
      ****************************************************
      *    Performed from 100-MAIN-MODULE, this routine  *
      ****************************************************

       200-GPA-RTN.
      ****************************************************
      * GPA'S % OF <2, >2 AND <3 AND >3 DECISION         *
      ****************************************************
           IF  GPA < 2
           THEN
               COMPUTE LOWR-THAN-TWO = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
           IF  GPA > 2  AND < 3
           THEN
               COMPUTE BTN-TWO-AND-THREE = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
           IF  GPA > 3
           THEN
               COMPUTE GRT-THEN-THREE= GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
      ******************************************************
      *  % OF STUDENTS WITH GPA'S GREATER THAN 3.0 IN      *
      *  BUS, LIB ARTS, ENG.                               *
      ******************************************************
           MOVE GRT-THEN-THREE TO TOTAL-SCL.

           IF SCL-CODE = '1'
           THEN
               COMPUTE BUS-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
           END-IF
           IF SCL-CODE = '2'
           THEN
               COMPUTE LIB-ARTS-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
           END-IF
           IF SCL-CODE = '3'
           THEN
                COMPUTE ENG-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
           END-IF

      ********************************************************
      *   STUDENTS WITH MORE THAN 100 CREDITS AND HAVE A GPA *
      *   OF LESS THAN 2.0%                                  *
      ********************************************************
           IF GPA > 2 AND CRD-EARNED < 100
           THEN
               COMPUTE MORE-THAN-HUND = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
      ********************************************************
      *    % OF STUDENTS WITH GPA GREATER THAN 3.0 WHO ARE   *
      *    FRESHMAN, SOPHOMORES, JUNNIORS, AND SENIORS       *
      ********************************************************
           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE= '1'
           THEN
               COMPUTE FRHMEN = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF

           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE = '2'
           THEN
               COMPUTE SPHMORS = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF

           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE= '3'
           THEN
               COMPUTE JUNRS = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF

           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE = '4'
           THEN
               COMPUTE SENRS = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
           WRITE OUTPUT-FILE.

 The data file ('C0805.DAT'):

234789543P. MARTINSON         11250011
276888003M. JACKSON           22394020

125654334C. HAYES             41400040

       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
      ****************************************************
      *   Main module controls overall program logic     *
      ****************************************************
       100-MAIN-MODULE.
           OPEN INPUT  STUDENT-FILE
                OUTPUT OUTPUT-FILE
      *    MOVE SPACES TO OUTPUT-FILE
           PERFORM UNTIL ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS = 'NO '
               READ STUDENT-FILE
                   AT END
                      MOVE 'NO ' TO ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS
                   NOT AT END
                      PERFORM 200-GPA-RTN
               END-READ
           END-PERFORM
           CLOSE STUDENT-FILE
                 OUTPUT-FILE
           STOP RUN.
      ****************************************************
      *    Performed from 100-MAIN-MODULE, this routine  *
      ****************************************************

       200-GPA-RTN.
      ****************************************************
      * GPA'S % OF <2, >2 AND <3 AND >3 DECISION         *
      ****************************************************
           IF  GPA < 2
           THEN
               COMPUTE LOWR-THAN-TWO = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
           IF  GPA > 2  AND < 3
           THEN
               COMPUTE BTN-TWO-AND-THREE = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
           IF  GPA > 3
           THEN
               COMPUTE GRT-THEN-THREE= GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
      ******************************************************
      *  % OF STUDENTS WITH GPA'S GREATER THAN 3.0 IN      *
      *  BUS, LIB ARTS, ENG.                               *
      ******************************************************
           MOVE GRT-THEN-THREE TO TOTAL-SCL.

           IF SCL-CODE = '1'
           THEN
               COMPUTE BUS-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
           END-IF
           IF SCL-CODE = '2'
           THEN
               COMPUTE LIB-ARTS-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
           END-IF
           IF SCL-CODE = '3'
           THEN
                COMPUTE ENG-MJR = TOTAL-SCL/SCL-CODE
           END-IF

      ********************************************************
      *   STUDENTS WITH MORE THAN 100 CREDITS AND HAVE A GPA *
      *   OF LESS THAN 2.0%                                  *
      ********************************************************
           IF GPA > 2 AND CRD-EARNED < 100
           THEN
               COMPUTE MORE-THAN-HUND = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
      ********************************************************
      *    % OF STUDENTS WITH GPA GREATER THAN 3.0 WHO ARE   *
      *    FRESHMAN, SOPHOMORES, JUNNIORS, AND SENIORS       *
      ********************************************************
           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE= '1'
           THEN
               COMPUTE FRHMEN = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF

           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE = '2'
           THEN
               COMPUTE SPHMORS = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF

           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE= '3'
           THEN
               COMPUTE JUNRS = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF

           IF  GPA < 3 AND CLS-CODE = '4'
           THEN
               COMPUTE SENRS = GPA-SUB-TOTAL / GPA
           END-IF
           WRITE OUTPUT-FILE.

 The data file ('C0805.DAT'):
125997600R. HENDERSON         11379011
234789543P. MARTINSON         11250011
276888003M. JACKSON           22394020
332557267B. BRUHANSKY         21314020
235654654K. THOMPSON          23279021
336221180F. SMITH             21304019
332557845D. MIKA              33217024
377000854A. ABRAMS            31379027
399000002L. NAIRN             33375029
211311411P. BUTTRAM           41397035
122886567F. DARK              41297034
224257889S. SMITH             41397038
125654334C. HAYES             41400040
345345669J. FISHER            42200036
432234543S. JONES             43291033
488345612M. PILLION           41255034
```

#### ↳ Re: Question

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-23T14:02:49-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b5l3te$m00$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<9f15a236.0303231121.5f1499d2@posting.google.com>`

```
The message

   "Operand SCL-CODE should  be numeric"

is your clue.  Comare this to

   SCL-CODE                        PIC X


That give you your answer.
```

### COBOL FAQ

- **From:** wmklein@ix.netcom.com (William M. Klein)
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T10:29:34+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol,news.answers,comp.answers
- **Message-ID:** `<computer-lang/cobol-faq_1048501737@rtfm.mit.edu>`

```
Archive-name: computer-lang/cobol-faq
Posting-Frequency: twice a month
Last-Modified: 2002/05/06
Version: 3.03
URL: http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm
Reply-To: wmklein@ix.netcom.com


                            COBOL FAQ
                  (Frequently Asked Questions)

Last-Modified: Spring 2002

The full COBOL FAQ can be viewed at:

  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send
private email to:

    wmklein <at> ix.netcom.com

Toward the top of the FAQ is a pointer to the appendix listing the most
recent significant updates and changes.

This FAQ answers such questions as (and addresses topics such as):

 - Where can I get a COBOL compiler? 
 - Is there a free COBOL compiler? 
 - Where can I contact... ?
 - What is happening with the draft of the next COBOL Standard and what
is in it? 
 - What about OO COBOL? 
 - Books about COBOL
 - COBOL Tools
 - What can/should I post in the COBOL newsgroups?
 - What about USAGE? COMP? Storage for data in xyz format? etc?
 - How do I get started with COBOL? Where can I get education?
Tutorials? Etc

and much, MUCH more
```

## Thread: NetExpress/ServerExpress - Oracle 9.2 upgrade - Gotcha's? — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### NetExpress/ServerExpress - Oracle 9.2 upgrade - Gotcha's?

- **From:** "tinman" <mikem@mrmsoft.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T11:31:47+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<D0Cfa.3319$rw.1436@nwrddc01.gnilink.net>`

```
We'll be upgrading Oracle from 8.1.7 to 9.2 in the near future. Anyone
aware of any "gotcha's" we might encounter? We're using NetExpress 3.1 and
ServerExpress 1.1.

Thanks
```

#### ↳ Re: NetExpress/ServerExpress - Oracle 9.2 upgrade - Gotcha's?

- **From:** "Simon Tobias" <Simon.Tobias@nospam.microfocus.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T12:28:03
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b5mtb4$u38$1@hyperion.microfocus.com>`
- **References:** `<D0Cfa.3319$rw.1436@nwrddc01.gnilink.net>`

```
> We'll be upgrading Oracle from 8.1.7 to 9.2 in the near future. Anyone
> aware of any "gotcha's" we might encounter? We're using NetExpress 3.1 and
> ServerExpress 1.1.

Oracle themselves have only certified Oracle 9i with Server Express 2.0.11
SP1 (or later) on UNIX, and NX 3.1 on Win32. That's not to say that earlier
versions of Server Express will or won't work, but there's no guarantees ...

Which flavours of UNIX are you working on? You may want to consider
upgrading to the latest release of Server Express. The Micro Focus UNIX
product matrix at
http://supportline.microfocus.com/productreleaselevels/unix.asp shows the
latest available versions for each platform.

Simon.
```

## Thread: Checking RACF for CICS TS — 5 this month / 6 total

_Topics: [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### Checking RACF for CICS TS

- **From:** jfry@ameritas.com (Jeff Fry)
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T08:42:18-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a381b426.0303240842.6bcf46b6@posting.google.com>`

```
We are migrating from APPC to TCP/IP.  I have defined a program in the
Security Exit for the CICS listener.  I am aware of the VERIFY
PASSWORD function to authenticate the uid/password.  What I am trying
to figure out is how to check RACF to make sure that the uid has
access to the transaction they are trying to issue.  So far, it
appears that a signon needs to be done.  Does anyone have examples of
how to do this?

Thanks,
Jeff
```

#### ↳ Re: Checking RACF for CICS TS

- **From:** "Roland Schiradin" <schiradi@tap.de>
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T23:23:26+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b5o09a$2m8m$1@innferno.news.tiscali.de>`
- **References:** `<a381b426.0303240842.6bcf46b6@posting.google.com>`

```
Jeff,

AFAIR. If you return the userid to the CSKL transaction CICS will issue
to checks:

1. Could the userid of transaction CSKL invoke transaction for userid
xxxxxx. RACF CL(SURROGAT) profile
DFHAPPL or DFHSTART
2. Is the userid correct (revoked or whatever)
3. Does the userid have access to this transaction.

This means CICS does already what you want/need

Roland

"Jeff Fry" <jfry@ameritas.com> schrieb im Newsbeitrag
news:a381b426.0303240842.6bcf46b6@posting.google.com...
> We are migrating from APPC to TCP/IP.  I have defined a program in the
> Security Exit for the CICS listener.  I am aware of the VERIFY
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> Jeff
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Checking RACF for CICS TS

- **From:** jfry@ameritas.com (Jeff Fry)
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T06:01:45-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a381b426.0303250601.11cb0450@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<a381b426.0303240842.6bcf46b6@posting.google.com> <b5o09a$2m8m$1@innferno.news.tiscali.de>`

```
I am returning the userid to the CSKL transaction.  When the userid I
pass back has access to the transaction through RACF, everything works
correctly.  If the userid does not have access, the listener doesn't
start the transaction.  It also does not respond to the client
process.  The client process just sits on the listen waiting for a
response until it times out.  Is there something I need to change to
get the CSKL transaction to respond to the client for this condition? 
Also, you mention RACF CL(SURROGAT) profile.  Are you indicating that
we would need to turn on Surrogate security for this?  Our security
administrators are very hesitant to do this.

Thanks,
Jeff


"Roland Schiradin" <schiradi@tap.de> wrote in message news:<b5o09a$2m8m$1@innferno.news.tiscali.de>...
> Jeff,
> 
…[24 more quoted lines elided]…
> > Jeff
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Checking RACF for CICS TS

- **From:** "Roland Schiradin" <schiradi@tap.de>
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T22:42:57+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b5qi9k$5oo$1@innferno.news.tiscali.de>`
- **References:** `<a381b426.0303240842.6bcf46b6@posting.google.com> <b5o09a$2m8m$1@innferno.news.tiscali.de> <a381b426.0303250601.11cb0450@posting.google.com>`

```
Jeff,

first problem:
Depending on a switch you return in your exit the CSKL will send a reply to
the client
or not. So it's your choice in the exit. Check the DfhCommArea layout for
the exit.

Second:
If the listener CSKL runs under userid XXXXX and try to start a transaction
FROG for
userid YYYYYY the XXXXXX need some surrogate permission to do so. It's *NOT*
SURROGATE at all its's just a profile like DFHSTART or DFHAPPL in
CL(SURROGAT).
Sorry I can't remember the exact name. Your security admin is right so
ensure userid XXXXXX (the CSKL userid)
can *NOT* be used via regular sign-on like userid for started tasks. A PLTPI
user or userid
of the started task might help. Bad effect of this is you can't start the
CSKL using the EZAO
transaction so you have to bounce the region. We do it the same to ensure
security and
also accounting as the accounting is based on the userid and we never had to
bounce the region
because of this for several years.

All it's not a Cobol problem and I don't read this newsgroup very often.

HTH
Roland

"Jeff Fry" <jfry@ameritas.com> schrieb im Newsbeitrag
news:a381b426.0303250601.11cb0450@posting.google.com...
> I am returning the userid to the CSKL transaction.  When the userid I
> pass back has access to the transaction through RACF, everything works
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
> "Roland Schiradin" <schiradi@tap.de> wrote in message
news:<b5o09a$2m8m$1@innferno.news.tiscali.de>...
> > Jeff,
> >
…[24 more quoted lines elided]…
> > > Jeff
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Checking RACF for CICS TS

- **From:** "Roland Schiradin" <schiradi@tap.de>
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T22:50:31+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b5qimt$5r8$1@innferno.news.tiscali.de>`
- **References:** `<a381b426.0303240842.6bcf46b6@posting.google.com> <b5o09a$2m8m$1@innferno.news.tiscali.de> <a381b426.0303250601.11cb0450@posting.google.com>`

```
Jeff,

if your CICS SIT contains a XUSER=NO the surrogate checking is not done,
but anyone can start transactions with a different userid. I don't know if
your
security admin like this also. Maybe they don't know anything about this
option:-)))

So decide yourself what to do

Roland

## Thread: Fujitsu ServiceWise Support - What has your experience been? — 7 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Fujitsu ServiceWise Support - What has your experience been?

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T16:50:31-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b5o23o$qlf$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
To any of you are "current" on your paid support for Fujitsu's COBOL product
and have used (or are currently using) their ServiceWise online support
service:

I submitted one problem on March 4th and another on March 5th.  By March
6th, ServiceWise support changed the status to "in development" (and I have
heard nothing to indicate that either US or Japan support/development have
had any problems recreating the software bugs).

On March 12th, I requested ANY update - even a status update or an estimate
on when I should EXPECT a status update.

Since my last ServiceWise update, it appears that NOTHING has happened - at
least I have seen nothing (even a request to "wait for 'x' amount of time
for a status update) from Fujitsu in my ServiceWise incident.

   ***

Is this type of "response" what others have found with ServiceWise reports
of software bugs?  If so, have you found any way to get more responsive
support and what was it?

Thank you in advance for any suggestions (other than switching vendors) that
you might have.
```

…[21 more quoted lines elided]…

I  'daresent' suggest you switch compilers <G>. But taking a leaf from the
opposition - the M/F Answer Exchange is working out quite well, and it is a
freebie. I think F/J should be offering something similar - where users raise
queries and they are either answered by the Tech development team or even
individual developers who have some background on the topic being raised.
Sometimes the reply may be,
"It will be in the next Fixpack or whatever", or, as you have done, "Raise this
as a
technical defect".

To be fair, there are times when you ask a question and the result is - SILENCE.

Jimmy, Calgary AB

I use ServiceWise for suggestions and oddities to which I don't care (much)
whether they answer.

For important stuff, I call 'em on the 'phone.

Also, support@adtools.com is read by the high-head muckety-muck boss.

I'm about to fire off a suggestion:

It seems as if (in PowerCOBOL), if you change a copy book, then ask for a
"Rebuild" (not "Rebuild All"), the project manager is not smart enough to
detect the copy book has been changed and does not recompile forms within a
project not otherwise changed. Imagine the grief when the copy book contains
EXTERNAL and is used by all the forms within the project. Some forms work
and some croak with a "WAtermelon-U-8-1-2-Green: The mayor's horse has
diabetes. Error occurred in module *unknown* because fleegum (codetype 2)
capacity exceeded 8192 portions. Address is 0C23F88DE127-g"
```

Is THAT what causes that error ...  been trying to find that one for months
...

You get the same error when the project manager does not recognize that the
"rep" files have changed, and the rebuild decides not to re-link certain
DLL's.  I've found that various obscure runtime errors dissappear
spontaneously when a full rebuild is done.  There is even the odd one that
requires both a rebuild all *and* a rebuild.

Donald

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu ServiceWise Support - What has your experience been?

- **From:** cobolnotdead@hotmail.com (Kate Parker)
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T06:12:28-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<49973e5b.0303250612.54b5a713@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<b5o23o$qlf$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Bill, while I have other complaints with ServiceWise, I have to say
that our response time from Alex and Lee at Fujitsu has generally been
outstanding (that's not to say that we always like the answer).  We
typically submit one to three incidents per month and most are
researched and resolved promptly.

My gripe has to do with the lack of "knowledge" found within the
ServiceWise Knowledge base.  I don't believe one of our reported
incidents have ever made it there, despite our requests.  I wish they
would take a look at other vendors, like Novell, and figure out what a
knowledge base is supposed to provide.  (On a positive note, I have
found that their sample programs deployed with the product and on
adtools.com are extremely useful for training.)

It also infuriates me when I find that they have existing patches for
errors in the product and it's only when I spend countless hours or
days trying to fix the problem that I become privy to these patches...
 I must have been mistaken when I thought a maintenance agreement
entitled me to patches and upgrades.

I would recommend emailing Ron Langer and your sales rep if you
haven't received the response you expect.

-Kate

> Is this type of "response" what others have found with ServiceWise reports
> of software bugs?  If so, have you found any way to get more responsive
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> you might have.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu ServiceWise Support - What has your experience been?

- **From:** robert@wagner.net (Robert Wagner)
- **Date:** 2003-03-26T02:01:37+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3e80fb5d.14744514@news.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<b5o23o$qlf$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net> <49973e5b.0303250612.54b5a713@posting.google.com>`

```
cobolnotdead@hotmail.com (Kate Parker) wrote:


>It also infuriates me when I find that they have existing patches for
>errors in the product and it's only when I spend countless hours or
>days trying to fix the problem that I become privy to these patches...
> I must have been mistaken when I thought a maintenance agreement
>entitled me to patches and upgrades.

I have no experience with ServiceWise or Fujitsu support (except I almost went
to work for them, another thread). I write to describe IBM's similar evolution
20-30 years earlier. 

In the beginning, IBM sent fixes to everyone 'on support'. They found it didn't
work well for one of three reasons, depending on whom you believe. 

1.  It caused more problems than it fixed. 
2.  It gave the impression that IBM software was full of bugs. 
3.  Users complained about needing to install almost daily fixes. 

So IBM changed to 'as needed' mode. If you queried the database or called,
they'd send the fix; otherwise, not .. until the next release. 

Pragmatically, it is probably the best solution.
```

### Variable Length Files- Fujitsu 5.0

- **From:** bj13th@netscape.net (BJ13)
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T15:52:15-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ef258d1a.0303241552.52939116@posting.google.com>`

```
I have some old programs that were used on MVS which need to be
adapted to Fujitsu Cobol 5.0 on the PC.  My problem is variable length
files that do not specify the length within the file.  The data file
contains two formats - A and B.  Format A had a record length of 500
which is followed by 1 to 99 format B records that have a length of
125.  These repeat.  The data is sequential (no delimiters).

Using the programs with the following two samples results in an
invalid delimiter error (inv-delmtr) when I compile the program.
Sample 1.
       FILE-CONTROL.
           SELECT INFILE ASSIGN TO FILEIN.
       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       FD  INFILE
           BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS
           RECORDING MODE IS V
           LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD.
       01  IN-RECORD.
           05  FILE-KEY
               10 FLD001-IN         PIC X(06).
               10 FLD002-IN         PIC X(05).
               10 FLD003-IN         PIC X(05). 
               10 FLD004-IN         PIC X(10).
           05  FORMAT-IN            PIC X(001).
           05  FILLER               PIC X(473).  
                         
       01  HISTORY-FMTA-RECORD      PIC X(500).  
       01  HISTORY-FMTB-RECORD      PIC X(125).

Sample 2.
       FILE-CONTROL.
            SELECT  INFILE ASSIGN TO FILEIN.
       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       FD  INFILE
           BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS
           RECORDING MODE IS V
           RECORD CONTAINS 125 TO 500 CHARACTERS
           LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
           DATA RECORDS ARE IN-RECORD
                            HISTORY-FMTA-RECORD
                            HISTORY-FMTB-RECORD. 

Read Section (same one used in both of above samples).

           250-READ SECTION.
           READ INFILE
               AT END MOVE 'YES' TO EOF-SW
               GO TO 250-EXIT.
           ADD 1 TO REC-READ-CTR.

           IF FORMAT-IN = 'A'
               MOVE HISTORY-FMTA-RECORD TO WS-FMTA-RECORD
               GO TO 250-EXIT.

           IF FORMAT-IN = 'B'
               MOVE HISTORY-FMTB-RECORD TO WS-FMTB-RECORD
           ELSE
               ADD 1 TO FORMAT-ERR-CTR.

Does anyone have an example of how these should be rewritten.  

Thanks
BJ
```

#### ↳ Re: Variable Length Files- Fujitsu 5.0

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T21:40:49-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<A6ednbfmOd6dTeKjXTWciA@giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<ef258d1a.0303241552.52939116@posting.google.com>`

```

"BJ13" <bj13th@netscape.net> wrote in message
news:ef258d1a.0303241552.52939116@posting.google.com...
> I have some old programs that were used on MVS which need to be
> adapted to Fujitsu Cobol 5.0 on the PC.  My problem is variable length
…[59 more quoted lines elided]…
> Does anyone have an example of how these should be rewritten.

First principles:

1. Your input file can't be #2 because Fujitsu's Variable Length files have
length descriptors on the front of each record (which your records don't).
Therefore, any attempt to use RECORDING MODE V is doomed.

2. Remember, in COBOL, one reads FILES. The sequential READ statement
accepts incoming data until a delimiter is hit OR the requested number of
bytes has been transferred.

3. Absent a delimiter, for the record pointer to point to the second record,
the first read operation must know the length of the first record and the
first read operation must know this length before starting the read.

4. In your #1, the first read operation transfers 500 bytes (the maximum
record length defined in the FD) and the record pointer for the next read is
sitting at byte 501. OK so far. Read #2 also transfers 500 bytes, but you
only want 125. Too bad. The record pointer is pointing to byte 1001 in
anticipation of read #3 - and you can't move the pointer back. Your trick of
moving 125 bytes out of the FD is useless; the compiler won't move the
residual 375 up to the front of the buffer.

5. Assuming the first record in the file is a type "A" record (you know its
length), you can set the length to read. If, after getting the "A" record,
you can figure out how many type "B" records follow, you're golden. Here's
how:

01  IN-REC.
    02  FILLER OCCURS 1 TO 500 DEPENDING ON LEN PIC X.

*>Get an "A"
PERFORM UNTIL EOF
  MOVE 500 TO LEN
  READ IN-FILE
  MOVE IN-REC TO WS-HEADER
  *> Somehow deduce number of "B" records following
  PERFORM VARYING RECNO FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL RECNO > NUM2DO
      MOVE 125 TO LEN
      READ IN-FILE
      MOVE IN-REC TO WS-DETAIL
      END-PERFORM
  END-PERFORM.

Without knowing how many "B" records follow each "A" record, you're pretty
much screwed.

Fortunately, there's an alternative: The CBL file routines.

Email me at jerry at-sign bisusa.com and I'll forward the source on some
subprograms to do what you need.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Variable Length Files- Fujitsu 5.0

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T22:46:14-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0303242246.b56896a@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<ef258d1a.0303241552.52939116@posting.google.com> <A6ednbfmOd6dTeKjXTWciA@giganews.com>`

```
"JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com> wrote 

> 5. Assuming the first record in the file is a type "A" record (you know its
> length), you can set the length to read. If, after getting the "A" record,
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>     02  FILLER OCCURS 1 TO 500 DEPENDING ON LEN PIC X.

If he is trying to read an existing file with 500 bytes then 125 bytes
then 125 etc then this cannot work.

Fujitsu will read this as if it is a variable format sequential file
that has record headers that includes the length.
```

#### ↳ Re: Variable Length Files- Fujitsu 5.0

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-03-24T22:35:21-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0303242235.2b37770a@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<ef258d1a.0303241552.52939116@posting.google.com>`

```
bj13th@netscape.net (BJ13) wrote 

> I have some old programs that were used on MVS which need to be
> adapted to Fujitsu Cobol 5.0 on the PC.  My problem is variable length
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> 125.  These repeat.  The data is sequential (no delimiters).

Are you trying to read an existing file, or you trying to change some
programs to use data files as you describe ?

If an existing file is just a block of 500 bytes followed by a
variable number of 125 byte records followd by another 500 byte record
then there is a problem and you can't define an FD that will usefully
describe this.

Because 500 is a multiple of 125 then it should be possible to read a
fixed record size of 125 bytes and build the 500 byte records in
memory from 4 reads.

For this define the file with just a single record of 125 bytes and
_no_ clause in the FD.  This will treat the data file as fixed chunks
125 bytes long (no headers, no delimiters).

Read the file, when you identity that a record is a 500 byte one do 3
more reads moving these 4 to W-S area.

If you are trying to emulate an existing program and the file will be
written by a Fujitsu program then use RECORD VARYING 125 TO 500 as a
clause on the FD (none other required).  Write appropriate record
descriptions and ensure that the type can be identified when it is
read.

The file will be written with headers that specify the length, but
this is not available in the program.

A READ AT END will read the variable record file and set the record
area to the appropriate data.  Identify the record to know which
record area data items to use.
```

#### ↳ Re: Variable Length Files- Fujitsu 5.0

- **From:** robert@wagner.net (Robert Wagner)
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T10:14:44+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3e802b6f.77349203@news.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<ef258d1a.0303241552.52939116@posting.google.com>`

```
bj13th@netscape.net (BJ13) wrote:

>I have some old programs that were used on MVS which need to be
>adapted to Fujitsu Cobol 5.0 on the PC.  My problem is variable length
>files that do not specify the length within the file. 

They DO specify the length within the file, on both mainframe and Fujitsu. On
the mainframe, there is a binary word in front of each record called the RSW
(Record Status Word). Fujitsu has something comparable. You are not aware it's
there because it doesn't appear in the COBOL record layout. It is created behind
the scenes when you do a WRITE. 

Your question asks about READ, but the answer lies in WRITE. Before you can READ
the variable length records in Fujitsu, you most have WRITTEN them in Fujitsu.
You didn't say how the file was created. 

Are you getting a compile or an execution error? I sounds like execution.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Variable Length Files- Fujitsu 5.0

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2003-03-26T22:29:27+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20030326172927.03943.00002768@mb-fj.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<3e802b6f.77349203@news.optonline.net>`

```
In article <3e802b6f.77349203@news.optonline.net>, robert@wagner.net (Robert
Wagner) writes:

>Your question asks about READ, but the answer lies in WRITE. Before you can
>READ
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>

If it was not written by Fujitsu, then it will not likely be read by fujitsu.
I tried migrating variable length files from the Host to the PC and it
definitely 
did not work very nicely at all.
You just about have to flatten out the records on the host to a common length,
get it FTP'ed in BINARY to prevent loss of packed fields then re-process
reading the
EBCDIC file into the PC ASCII variable length file.
At least that is the long way around the horse that I had to exercise.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Variable Length Files- Fujitsu 5.0

- **From:** robert@wagner.net (Robert Wagner)
- **Date:** 2003-03-27T03:23:51+00:00

- **Message-ID:** `<3e8249c2.100362261@news.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<3e802b6f.77349203@news.optonline.net> <20030326172927.03943.00002768@mb-fj.aol.com>`

```
sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky) wrote:

>In article <3e802b6f.77349203@news.optonline.net>, robert@wagner.net (Robert
>Wagner) writes:
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…
>At least that is the long way around the horse that I had to exercise.

I suggested that as the tedious approach. There _are_ tools which can do the
transfer in one step, but most people don't have them. This why we recommend
formatting all files in display, without comp-3 or binary. Because it makes
transfer to another platform so much easier. 

Have you learned your lesson? Are your PC files display? 

Robert
```

## Thread: Fujitsu Cobol in a Network — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Fujitsu Cobol in a Network

- **From:** "pty0" <xxx@clix.pt>
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T09:45:35
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<FzVfa.262$Ux7.188516@newsserver.ip.pt>`

```

I would like to know which is the best way to install the runtime of Power
Cobol or the application from the server, instead of going to every
workstation and install it one by one, or which is the best way to install
an application (including the runtimes) in a network

Thanks
Joe
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu Cobol in a Network

- **From:** thaneh@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T07:41:59-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bfdfc3e8.0303250741.3c287cc9@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<FzVfa.262$Ux7.188516@newsserver.ip.pt>`

```
Fujitsu offers a Network Installer.  This can be used to install the
runtime files on the server, but each workstation must run a
subinstall - from the server- that sets the necessary registry
entries.

Contact Fujitsu support about the Network installer.

## Thread: Find job-name etc in a cobol program? — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Jobs, careers, recruiting, salary`](topics.md#jobs)_

### Find job-name etc in a cobol program?

- **From:** Einar Clementz <eclementz@bigfoot.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T15:02:06+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3E80615E.3030908@bigfoot.com>`

```
Hi.

I remember once seeing a cobol program on this group that found the 
MVS-id, job-name etc. Now I need this feature and cannot find back to it.

Does anyone know about how this can be done?


Thanx, Einar Clementz, Oslo, Norway
```

#### ↳ Re: Find job-name etc in a cobol program?

- **From:** SkippyPB <swiegand@neo.NOSPAM.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T11:11:19-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<DC9CDA8FDD9D3697.DD616700C0FCAAA1.8A01390C9EEF31CD@lp.airnews.net>`
- **References:** `<3E80615E.3030908@bigfoot.com>`

```
On Tue, 25 Mar 2003 15:02:06 +0100, Einar Clementz
<eclementz@bigfoot.com> enlightened us:

>Hi.
>
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>Thanx, Einar Clementz, Oslo, Norway

Originally written by CLC member Gilbert Saint-flour, here it is:

      Identification Division.
         Program-ID. Cob2Job.
         Author. Gilbert Saint-flour. 
       Data Division.
        Working-Storage Section.
         01 job-name Pic x(8).
         01 proc-step Pic x(8).
         01 step-name Pic x(8).
         01 job-number Pic x(8).
         01 program-name Pic x(8).
         01 user-id Pic x(8).
         01 group-name Pic x(8).
         01 user-name Pic x(20).
         01 four-bytes. 05 full-word Pic s9(8) Comp.
        Linkage Section.
         01 cb1.  05 ptr1 Pointer Occurs 256.
         01 cb2.  05 ptr2 Pointer Occurs 256.
       Procedure Division.
 PSA       SET Address Of cb1 To NULL.
 TCB       SET Address Of cb1 To ptr1(136)
 TIOT      SET Address Of cb2 To ptr1(4)
           MOVE cb2(1:8) To job-name
           MOVE cb2(9:8) To proc-step
           MOVE cb2(17:8) To step-name
 JSCB      SET Address Of cb2 To ptr1(46)
           MOVE cb2(361:8) To program-name
 SSIB      SET Address Of cb2 To ptr2(80)
           MOVE cb2(13:8) To job-number
 PSA       SET Address Of cb1 To NULL.
 ASCB      SET Address Of cb1 To ptr1(138)
 ASXB      SET Address Of cb2 To ptr1(28)
           MOVE cb2(193:8) To user-id
 ACEE      SET Address Of cb2 To ptr2(51)
           MOVE cb2(31:8) To group-name
 UNAM      SET Address Of cb1 To ptr2(26)
           MOVE zero to full-word.
           MOVE cb1(1:1) To four-bytes(4:1)
           MOVE cb1(2:full-word) To user-name
           DISPLAY job-name ' '
                   proc-step ' '
                   step-name ' '
                   job-number ' '
                   program-name ' '
                   user-id ' '
                   group-name  ' '
                   user-name  ' '
           GOBACK.

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

Micro$oft Haiku Error Message #111

The Tao that is seen
Is not the true Tao - until
You bring fresh toner.
 
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Remove nospam to email me.

Steve
```

- **References:** `<3E80615E.3030908@bigfoot.com> <DC9CDA8FDD9D3697.DD616700C0FCAAA1.8A01390C9EEF31CD@lp.airnews.net>`

### xml --&gt; cobol conversion

- **From:** Lindsey <member27082@dbforums.com>

## Thread: Convert MainFrame Data [ EBCDIC ] To Ascii — 131 this month / 1161 total

_Topics: [`Migration and conversion`](topics.md#migration) · [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### Convert MainFrame Data [ EBCDIC ] To Ascii

- **From:** va_bala_reddy@rediffmail.com (Jim George)
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T07:04:30-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<45147cf3.0303250704.6a7dd28@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi!
I have EBCDIC data from an IBM Mainframe which I need to convert to
ASCII Format.
I have managed to convert the remaining data into the proper format
but am stuck due to COMP-3.
There are fields as follows:
Seq Dt S9(7)
Seq Period X(2)
both of which are in a group.
How do i convert them into ASCII format ?

Any help is truly appreciated.

Thanks
Jim
```

#### ↳ Re: Convert MainFrame Data [ EBCDIC ] To Ascii

- **From:** Binyamin Dissen <postingid@dissensoftware.com>
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T17:33:53+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<eit08v87gleb87s0nv66unaceaa7ir094v@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<45147cf3.0303250704.6a7dd28@posting.google.com>`

```
On 25 Mar 2003 07:04:30 -0800 va_bala_reddy@rediffmail.com (Jim George) wrote:

:>I have EBCDIC data from an IBM Mainframe which I need to convert to
:>ASCII Format.
:>I have managed to convert the remaining data into the proper format
:>but am stuck due to COMP-3.
:>There are fields as follows:
:>Seq Dt S9(7)
:>Seq Period X(2)
:>both of which are in a group.
:>How do i convert them into ASCII format ?

1. X(2) is probably not COMP-3 (if it is, shoot the programmer).

2. COMP cannot be converted to ascii - it must be shipped as it. If you wish
to ship the data as ascii, write a program that copies the numerics to USAGE
DISPLAY fields and ship the expanded records.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Convert MainFrame Data [ EBCDIC ] To Ascii

- **From:** Colin Campbell <cmcampb@attglobal.net>
- **Date:** 2003-03-25T09:26:46-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3E809156.1C1F8A8A@attglobal.net>`
- **References:** `<45147cf3.0303250704.6a7dd28@posting.google.com> <eit08v87gleb87s0nv66unaceaa7ir094v@4ax.com>`

```
I agree with Binyamin.  Both EBCDIC and ASCII are intended to handle characters,
not specially encoded data.

The wise course is to write a super simple program on the mainframe.  It will use
the COPY member that describes the original data (you do use COPY members for all
files, right?), and a modified version of the COPY member where all of the data is
in character format.  The numeric fields, if they require a sign, should be
described as SIGN TRAILING SEPARATE.  (You could also use a Numeric Edited PICture
string for the output numeric fields if you prefer.)  The program reads an input
record, moves each elementary item to the output record (CORRESPONDING will work),
and writes the output record.

Transmit the output to the ASCII platform, and you're done.  No nasty surprises,
no errors.

Just another application of the KISS principle.

Binyamin Dissen wrote:

> On 25 Mar 2003 07:04:30 -0800 va_bala_reddy@rediffmail.com (Jim George) wrote:
>
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
> Director, Dissen Software, Bar & Grill - Israel
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Convert MainFrame Data [ EBCDIC ] To Ascii

- **From:** robert@wagner.net (Robert Wagner)
- **Date:** 2003-03-26T02:01:43+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3e8104c2.17149379@news.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<45147cf3.0303250704.6a7dd28@posting.google.com> <eit08v87gleb87s0nv66unaceaa7ir094v@4ax.com> <3E809156.1C1F8A8A@attglobal.net>`

```
Colin Campbell <cmcampb@attglobal.net> wrote:

>I agree with Binyamin.  Both EBCDIC and ASCII are intended to handle
characters,
>not specially encoded data.
>
>The wise course is to write a super simple program on the mainframe.  It will
use
>the COPY member that describes the original data (you do use COPY members for
all
>files, right?), and a modified version of the COPY member where all of the data
is
>in character format.  The numeric fields, if they require a sign, should be
>described as SIGN TRAILING SEPARATE.  (You could also use a Numeric Edited
PICture
>string for the output numeric fields if you prefer.)  The program reads an
input
>record, moves each elementary item to the output record (CORRESPONDING will
work),
>and writes the output record.
>
>Transmit the output to the ASCII platform, and you're done.  No nasty
surprises,
>no errors.
>
>Just another application of the KISS principle.

No, the KISS principle says create the file in portable format in the first
place. Then, when someone calls to ask where he can get your file, you respond
'it's on server X at \dir'. Seconds later, he has it 

It works a lot better than saying, 'I'll have to write a program to export it.'
```

it also contains records he had deleted in the application and doesn't have 
some new records and there are blocks of junk data.

And that is just if it was MF C-ISAM or Level II.  With IDXFORMAT"3" or 
Fujitsu ISAM he would at least not even been able to make a start.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Convert MainFrame Data [ EBCDIC ] To Ascii

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-03-26T15:08:39-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0303261508.4e59f74a@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<45147cf3.0303250704.6a7dd28@posting.google.com> <eit08v87gleb87s0nv66unaceaa7ir094v@4ax.com> <3E809156.1C1F8A8A@attglobal.net> <3e8104c2.17149379@news.optonline.net> <b5r7p8$mc0$1@aklobs.kc.net.nz> <3e81df29.73068412@news.optonline.net>`

```
robert@wagner.net (Robert Wagner) wrote 

> >> No, the KISS principle says create the file in portable format in the
> >> first place. Then, when someone calls to ask where he can get your file,
> >> you respond
> >> 
> >> 'it's on server X at \dir'. Seconds later, he has it

> >4 hours later he is complaining that he has wasted an afternoon 

> You give him a flat file created by your last reorg.

Ohh, so you give him an _old_ version of the data that may be last
month's,
or even months ago.

Actually I don't have a 'flat file from the last reorg', That implies
that there is a two pass mechanism.  I build a new indexed file from
the data file of the original and then rename the old files to some
junk name, rename the new file to the actual file name and delete the
junk names.

This runs faster and is (IMHO) less prone to failures. If there are
any fails then one is left with the original file unchanged or all
files have the wrong name (but still exists).

A copy to flat file and back to the original file may fail while
writing back and could leave the file incomplete.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Convert MainFrame Data [ EBCDIC ] To Ascii

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** robert@wagner.net (Robert Wagner)
- **Date:** 2003-03-27T03:23:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3e824d0f.101207362@news.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<45147cf3.0303250704.6a7dd28@posting.google.com> <eit08v87gleb87s0nv66unaceaa7ir094v@4ax.com> <3E809156.1C1F8A8A@attglobal.net> <3e8104c2.17149379@news.optonline.net> <b5r7p8$mc0$1@aklobs.kc.net.nz> <3e81df29.73068412@news.optonline.net> <217e491a.0303261508.4e59f74a@posting.google.com>`

```
riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard) wrote:

>robert@wagner.net (Robert Wagner) wrote 
>
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>or even months ago.

Such requests are usually for testing format compatibility, or for reference
files which don't often change. If he needs a current version, I say "give me
five minutes to reorg it".

>Actually I don't have a 'flat file from the last reorg', That implies
>that there is a two pass mechanism.  I build a new indexed file from
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>writing back and could leave the file incomplete.

You should leave the original with its original name until the reorg is
successful. When the reorg finishes without error, THEN rename the original to
junk and the new version to the original name. 

Granted, a two pass process is slower. But its by-product is a flat file. It
would be helpful to have two reorg scripts. If you live in an isolated world,
use .idx to .idx as your default. If you live in my world, where such calls are
frequent, use two pass as your default.
```

> You should leave the original with its original name until the reorg is

original to
> junk and the new version to the original name.
